content: We can refactor the ReAct-Reflexion reasoning framework to dynamically gate its reflection and action planning cycles around the agent's working memory capacity limit: only high-uncertainty reasoning traces that do not exceed working memory bounds are passed to Reflexion for iterative self-correction, while low-uncertainty traces are truncated to free working memory for immediate action. This capacity-aware gating maintains the interpretable structured reasoning of ReAct-Reflexion while preventing performance collapse from working memory overload.
source_topics: agent planning reasoning frameworks ReAct Reflexion
agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent planning reasoning frameworks ReAct Reflexion
